steps.title: Clear the fractions
steps.content:
Start with
$$
\frac{1}{6xy}+xyz=\frac{1}{5yz}
$$
Since $x$, $y$, and $z$ are positive, multiply every term by $30xyz$.
$$
5z+30x^2y^2z^2=6x
$$

steps.title: Isolate the square
steps.content:
Move $5z$ to the right side, then divide by $30x^2z^2$.
$$
30x^2z^2y^2=6x-5z
$$
$$
y^2=\frac{6x-5z}{30x^2z^2}
$$

steps.title: Take the positive root
steps.content: Because $y$ is positive, use the positive square root. $$ y=\sqrt{\frac{6x-5z}{30x^2z^2}} $$ The answer is B.
